message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): HTTP/1.0 response decoding contract (tick 404)

Add `distributed_http_client_decodes_http_1_0_response` to the
ft-xbnl0.2.4 HTTP-client contract surface.

Why: older servers, embedded HTTP stacks, and several reverse proxies
still emit responses whose status line begins with `HTTP/1.0` rather
than `HTTP/1.1`. A client that only parses the `HTTP/1.1` shape would
hard-fail on first contact with such a peer, even when everything else
about the response (Content-Length, body framing, Connection: close)
is well-formed. Pinning the HTTP/1.0 parse path as a contract prevents
a regression that would otherwise only surface against real third-party
peers.

Test shape (matches tick-402 chunked-transfer-encoding test):
  1. Bind a TcpListener on 127.0.0.1:0 and hand the accepted socket
     to an asupersync::task::spawn worker.
  2. Worker reads the request once (drain), then writes back a fixed
     response:
         HTTP/1.0 200 OK\r\n
         Content-Length: 6\r\n
         Connection: close\r\n
         \r\n
         legacy
     and calls `stream.shutdown(Both)` to close the socket cleanly.
  3. Client side uses `DistributedHttpClient::plaintext()` with a
     fresh `asupersync::cx::Cx::for_testing()` to issue a GET against
     http://127.0.0.1:PORT/legacy.
  4. Assert `resp.status == 200` and `resp.body == b"legacy"`, then
     `join()` the server task.

Feature-gated behind `distributed` (same as surrounding tests) and
wrapped in `run_async_test` to match the existing conventions in
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
